patches_applied.fix: Restated the formula as (f^-1)'(b) = 1 / f'(f^-1(b)) and added two explicit conditions: (1) f must be one-to-one (monotonic) on the interval so that f^-1 exists, and (2) f'(f^-1(b)) must be non-zero — otherwise f has a horizontal tangent there and f^-1 has a vertical tangent (slope undefined). Both the content_hint and narration_seed now carry the caveat.

Replaced the terse '|x| < 1' footnote with an explicit domain clarification: the FUNCTION arcsin x is defined on the closed interval [-1, 1], but its DERIVATIVE 1/sqrt(1 - x^2) is only defined on the OPEN interval (-1, 1) because the denominator sqrt(1 - x^2) vanishes at x = +/-1, producing vertical tangents at the endpoints. Same clarification applied to arccos; arctan flagged as differentiable everywhere. Both content_hint and narration_seed updated.

Added a one-line footnote to content_hint and a matching sentence to narration_seed noting that the slope dy/dx = -(2x + y)/(x + 2y) is undefined wherever x + 2y = 0, which is the locus of vertical tangents on the curve x^2 + xy + y^2 = 7 (denominator vanishes).

Added a worked implicit-second-derivative beat for x^2 + y^2 = 25: starting from dy/dx = -x/y, applying the quotient rule, substituting dy/dx back in, and simplifying via the original equation to get d^2y/dx^2 = -25/y^3. Both content_hint and narration_seed now include this beat plus the meta-lesson that implicit d^2y/dx^2 always requires substituting the first derivative back in.

Added a blended worked example to both content_hint and narration_seed: d/dx[x * sin(x^2)] = sin(x^2) + 2x^2 * cos(x^2), which nests a chain rule inside a product rule. Also added the meta-prompt 'identify the OUTERMOST structure first, then peel inward' so students see how nesting actually works under time pressure.

rationale: All five minor-severity patches from round 1 (one from Reviewer A on inverse-trig domain wording, four from Reviewer B on the inverse-function caveat, vertical-tangent footnote, implicit higher-order beat, and blended selecting-procedures example) have been incorporated into both the content_hint and narration_seed fields of the affected sections, with consistent updates propagated to the module-level specific_examples list. No new factual errors, accreditation claims, or scope creep were introduced; all other sections are unchanged and the JSON remains valid. The outline is ready to ship to the video-production pipeline.
